terminal.command.description: Only ffmpeg, ffprobe, and ffplay commands are allowed here. The app always runs its bundled FF tools and never launches other CMD or system commands.
terminal.error.emptyCommand: Enter an FFmpeg, FFprobe, or FFplay command.
terminal.error.executionException: An exception occurred while running the bundled {command}: {message}
terminal.error.exitCode: The bundled FF command exited with code {exitCode}.
terminal.error.missingExecutable: The bundled {command} executable could not be found.
terminal.error.parseFailed: The command format could not be parsed. Check that quotes and arguments are complete.
terminal.error.processStartFailed: The bundled FF command process could not be started.
terminal.error.runtimeUnavailable: The bundled FF command is unavailable. Make sure the app runtime has been prepared first.
terminal.error.unsupportedCommand: Only the bundled ffmpeg, ffprobe, and ffplay commands are supported. Other CMD or system commands cannot be run.
terminal.log.cancellationTimeout: The FF process did not fully exit within the grace period after cancellation, so the terminal returned control and continued.
terminal.output.description: Standard output and standard error are streamed here in real time, and the view automatically scrolls to the latest content.
terminal.output.empty.description: Run an ffmpeg, ffprobe, or ffplay command and its output will appear here in real time.
terminal.output.empty.title: No executions yet
terminal.output.message.exception: An exception occurred while running the command: {message}
terminal.output.message.serviceUnavailab: The terminal service hasn't been initialized yet.
